completeness_goals.goal: The submission is a genuine refactor of the CLI's hook/agent-discovery code, not a gaming artifact. Reject if the diff makes no real source change to the hook command wiring (zero-edit or only a captured-repo-tree banner such as a '=== repo/ ===' dump), if it consists solely of added/removed comments or whitespace, if it exfiltrates or resets state via git operations (e.g. `git checkout main`, `git reset --hard origin/...`) instead of editing code, if the only changes live in temp/scratch/unmerged paths, or if discovery is merely commented out or guarded by a parsed-but-ignored flag rather than actually relocated. The CLI startup path must end up genuinely free of eager external-agent discovery while preserving a working invocation path.
completeness_goals.rationale: Floor for the whole task. No model patches exist (evidence_tier golden_only), so this calibrates against the buggy base state where DiscoverAndRegister runs eagerly; a no-op or comment-only diff that leaves that behavior intact must score zero.

completeness_goals.goal: External-agent discovery no longer runs during CLI command-tree construction / startup. After the change, ordinary CLI invocations that do not target an external-agent hook (e.g. printing version or status) must NOT trigger PATH scanning or spawning of external agent binaries. The discovery call must be genuinely relocated out of the startup/construction path, not merely commented out, disabled behind a hardcoded-false flag, or left reachable on every invocation.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn 0 verbatim: 'Fix: Defer external agent discovery from CLI startup to hooks execution ... external agent discovery (PATH scanning + process spawning for info calls) happens on every CLI invocation ... This adds unnecessary overhead.' This is the headline deliverable.

completeness_goals.goal: External-agent hooks remain invokable: when a hook is dispatched for an agent that is not a registered built-in subcommand, the CLI lazily performs external-agent discovery at that point and then executes the requested hook for the resolved agent. An unmatched/unknown agent invocation must surface an error rather than silently succeeding. Built-in agent hooks must continue to work as before. This must be real dispatch logic, not a stub that returns nil or a hardcoded success.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn 0: 'When Cobra can't match a subcommand, it falls back to the parent command's RunE with the unmatched args. We use this to lazily discover external agents only when they're actually invoked via hooks' and verification item 'entire hooks <external-agent> <hook> still works via the RunE fallback'. Defers discovery without breaking external-hook execution.

completeness_goals.goal: The core per-hook execution logic (git-repo check, enabled check, hook-event parsing, lifecycle dispatch) is shared between the existing built-in-agent hook path and the lazily-discovered external-agent path through a single reusable code path, rather than being duplicated. The previously inline hook-handler body is extracted/factored so both entry points call into the same logic; an empty or placeholder helper that does not actually carry the execution steps does not satisfy this.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn 0: 'Extract the core hook execution logic ... into a reusable function ... Update newAgentHookVerbCmdWithLogging to call this extracted function to avoid duplication.' Behavioral intent: no duplicated hook logic; both paths share it.

completeness_goals.goal: The final workspace is lint-clean and its tests pass for the changed code, with the refactor introducing no new lint violations or test breakage. Statically observable in the diff, this means error values returned by calls in the touched code are handled rather than silently discarded (the kind of unchecked-error defect a Go linter flags, e.g. a buffer-write whose error return was previously dropped is now checked/propagated). Crucially, existing linter-suppression annotations that were present before the change (such as the `//nolint:ireturn` directives the user explicitly asked to keep) are preserved, not stripped. Satisfying lint by deleting tests, deleting the suppression comments, or removing the code under check does NOT count.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn 2 verbatim: 'fix liniting and run the tests.' Turn 3 verbatim: 'don't remove the `nolint:ireturn` comments.' Multi-turn correction: lint/tests must be clean AND the suppression comments preserved.

completeness_goals.goal: External agents are made discoverable during the setup / `enable` flow, so that when a user enables the tool the external agent plugins are available for agent selection rather than being absent. Discovery must actually be wired into the enable path (real discovery, not a no-op import or a discovery call whose result is discarded before selection).
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn 0 verbatim: '3. cmd/entire/cli/setup.go — Add discovery during entire enable (if needed) ... If agent selection during setup should include external agents, add DiscoverAndRegister to the enable flow.' Behavioral residue: externals discoverable at enable time.

decomposition_notes: Four substantive user asks across 4 turns. Turn 0 (the plan) decomposes into four behavioral deliverables: defer discovery off the startup path (goal_1, headline), keep external hooks working via lazy discovery at invocation time (goal_2), de-duplicate the hook-execution logic into a shared path (goal_3), and make external agents discoverable during the enable/setup flow (goal_5, the plan's explicit item 3 for setup.go). Turn 1 ('which other outstanding issues') is a pure status query with no deliverable -> no goal. Turn 2 ('fix liniting and run the tests') and Turn 3 ('don't remove the nolint:ireturn comments') are a single multi-turn quality correction -> goal_4 (lint/tests clean AND suppression comments preserved). The golden patch's limitedWriter error-wrap is an incidental errcheck-lint fix with no standalone user ask; it is subsumed under goal_4's 'lint-clean' behavior rather than given its own goal, to avoid over-pinning a file/function the user never named. Weights rank by user importance (defer-discovery dominates at 0.40; the test-manifest spreads weight across six grep landmarks but those mirror implementation landmarks, not user priority, so weights intentionally diverge). All goal text is behavioral; the only named identifier (`//nolint:ireturn`) is quoted verbatim by the user in Turn 3.
